title: Star Wars: Legacy
content:

{{Quote|It's ROOTED in SW tradition but. . .it's a whole new era.|[[John Ostrander]]|<ref name="JCF new era">
{{JCFCite|url=posts/23037928|board=Literature|user=jfostrader|thread=The Official Legacy Series Discussion Thread|date=2006-02-01|time=9:37 am|archivedate=20230724190958}}</ref>}}
'''''Star Wars: Legacy''''' is a [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series written by [[John Ostrander]], primarily pencilled and co-scripted by [[Jan Duursema]], and inked by [[Dan Parsons]]. Published by [[Dark Horse Comics]], ''Legacy''{{'s}} first issues were released in June [[2006]] following Dark Horse's revamp of its ''[[Star Wars]]'' line earlier in the year. Its initial tagline was "''All new [[One Sith|Sith Order]]! All new [[Fel Empire|Empire]]! All new [[Cade Skywalker|Skywalker]]!''"

The series is set in the [[Legacy era]], predominantly at [[137 ABY]]. Its chief protagonist is [[Cade Skywalker]], a descendant of the [[Skywalker family/Legends|Skywalker family]] who has given up his [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] heritage to pursue a life as a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]]. Cade's story is set against the backdrop of a [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] in a state of [[Second Imperial Civil War|civil war]], with a [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] seated on the Imperial throne seeing his power being challenged by [[New Jedi Order|many]] [[Empire-in-exile|different]] [[Galactic Alliance Remnant|forces]].

''Legacy'' delved nearly 100 years further into the ''Star Wars'' timeline than any previously published material. Despite some initial uproar upon its announcement in 2006, it went on to become one of the most well-received and popular [[Star Wars Legends|Expanded Universe]] works of all time, as well as one of Dark Horse's best-selling comic series.

The series ran for 50 issues, ending with the release of [[Legacy (2006) 50|''Legacy'' (2006) 50]] on [[August 18]], [[2010]], with an additional six-part series ''[[Star Wars: Legacy — War]]'' from December 2010 to [[May 25]], [[2011]]. On [[December 3]], [[2012]], Dark Horse Comics announced the return of the ''Star Wars: Legacy'' series with a [[Star Wars: Legacy Volume 2|second iteration]] featuring the great-great granddaughter of [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]].<ref>{{WebCite|url=https://comicbook.com/news/star-wars-legacy-returns-to-dark-horse-comics|text=Star Wars: Legacy Returns to Dark Horse Comics|date=2017-09-06|work=comicbook|archivedate=20200920111046}}</ref><ref>{{WebCite|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180910175431/https://www.cbr.com/exclusive-star-wars-legacy-returns-in-2013|text=EXCLUSIVE: "Star Wars: Legacy" Returns In 2013|date=2012-12-03|work=Comic Book Resources|archivedate=20200630052023}}</ref> Following Lucasfilm's sale to The Walt Disney Company and the return of the Star Wars comic license to [[Marvel Comics]], ''Star Wars: Legacy'' was cancelled.

==Development==
===Conception===
{{Quote|To celebrate its own 15th anniversary of publishing Star Wars in comic form, Randy had convinced everyone at DH to cancel the existing books and start afresh with new ones – including Legacy. The franchise does pretty well for DH so, to my mind, that was a gutsy gamble. From the looks of the panel, I'd say the gamble has paid off.|John Ostrander on the Dark Horse panel at [[Celebration IV]]|<ref name="ComicMix">{{WebCite|url=https://www.comicmix.com/2007/05/31/john-ostrander-star-wars-c4la|text=JOHN OSTRANDER: Star Wars C4LA|author=John Ostrander|date=2007-05-31|archivedate=20220707071227}}</ref>}}
In the 1990s, Dark Horse produced a number of stories set after [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']], most notably ''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire]]'', the [[Star Wars: X-Wing Rogue Squadron|''X-Wing'']] series, and ''[[Star Wars: Union]]''. However, from [[1998]] to [[2005]], its flagship series were [[Star Wars: Republic|''Republic'']] and [[Star Wars: Empire|''Empire'']], and stories set in either the [[New Republic era]] or the [[New Jedi Order era]] were rare, appearing only as short stories in the [[Star Wars Tales|''Tales'']] series. When it began, the closest ''Star Wars'' comic to ''Legacy'' in the timeline was "[[Equals & Opposites]]," set more than 100 years before the beginning of ''Legacy''.

The idea for the series came from either [[John Ostrander]] or [[Randy Stradley]], and artist and co-plotter [[Jan Duursema]] had to be sold on it. Afterwards, it was shown to [[Shelly Shapiro]], so it could be coordinated with ''[[Star Wars: Legacy of the Force]]'', a book series which was soon to be published.<ref>{{JCFCite|url=posts/23170186|board=Literature|thread=The Official Legacy Series Discussion Thread|user=[[John Ostrander|jfostrander]]|date=2006-02-15|time=8:22 am|archivedate=20200801160707}}</ref>

[[File:Legacyteam.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Four members of the ''Legacy'' team at the [[2007]] New York Comic Con: Randy Stradley, John Ostrander, Sean Cooke, and Jan Duursema.]] The first official announcement of Legacy came at newsarama.com.<ref>{{WebCite|url=http://forum.newsarama.com/showthread.php?s=d4bd6b2bc95c8e4611e9113e9402bc45&threadid=57855|text=DARK HORSE ANNOUNCES STAR WARS: LEGACY|work=Newsrama Forums|archivedate=20100821011334}}</ref> Immediately following this announcement, fan reaction was very vocal, and very mixed.<ref>{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=245230&start=0#13983153|thread=Star Wars: Legacy comic series SPECULATION (*possible SPOILERS*)|user=James T. Skywalker|date=2006-02-01|time=12:56 PM}}</ref><ref name="John Jan" /><ref>{{JCFCite|url=posts/23037764|board=Literature|thread=The Official Legacy Series Discussion Thread|archivedate=20150915204245}}</ref> Some readers were excited at the prospect of a series set 100 years in the future in completely uncharted territory, while others were worried about seemingly stereotypical characters (such as the revival of the Sith) and plotlines, and that the series would destroy [[Del Rey]]'s continuity while also inhibiting future novel storylines. John Ostrander and Jan Duursema were very active on popular Star Wars forums, answering fans' questions and attempting to assuage their concerns.

On the [[StarWars.com Message Boards]], [[Sue Rostoni]] and [[Leland Chee]] assured skeptical fans that everyone at both [[Lucasfilm]] and Del Rey were on board for the series, and that the backstory had even been run by the authors of ''[[Star Wars: Legacy of the Force]]''.<ref>{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=222689&start=1455|thread=Books, Comics, & Television VIPs|nobackup=1}}</ref> Similarly, John Ostrander stated that he and Jan Duursema were taking into account what was happening in ''Legacy of the Force'' and working with it.<ref>{{JCFCite|url=posts/23141710|board=Literature|thread=The Official Legacy Series Discussion Thread|user=[[John Ostrander|jfostrander]]|date=2006-02-12|time=1:32 pm|archivedate=20200801160544}}</ref> Rostoni expressed her doubts that it would impede the storyline of ''Legacy of the Force'' in any way,<ref>{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=222689&start=1470#14050122|thread=Books, Comics, & Television VIPs|user=[[Sue Rostoni|Eeusu Estornii]]|date=2007-02-09|time=1:18 PM}}</ref> and Chee pointed out that there are large story gaps between the two series.<ref name="SWBoards 2190">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=222689&start=2190#14975722|thread=Books, Comics, & Television VIPs|user=wild~karrde|date=2006-06-07|time=9:52 AM|quote=Also keep in mind that there are humongous story gaps between the two; don't expect answers to everything right away. Both series were created to be enjoyed independently of the other. Consider any direct links between the two as gravy.}}</ref> He did mention, however:
{{Quote|There were definitely things that we told both of them to stay clear of because of things the other team was doing. Even with the large gap, there are some things that the comic team needed direction on such as what would the Jedi academy look like? What has happened to Coruscant?|Leland Chee|<ref name="SWBoards 2190" />}}
During the making of ''[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed]]'', it was revealed that one of the initial ideas for the [[Star Wars video games|video game]] had been a game set 500 years in the future, with the player being "The last Skywalker", though such idea was ultimately dropped in favor of [[Galen Marek]]'s story.<ref name="LucasArts-The Force Unleashed - From Concept to Console">{{LucasArts|url=games/theforceunleashed/gameinfo/diaries/diary20070323.html|text=The Force Unleashed - From Concept to Console}}</ref> This concept is very similar to what eventually became ''Legacy'', though it is doubtful that this is anything more than a coincidence.

===="Legacy" in the title====
[[File:Legacy_characters.jpg|thumb|right|350px|Legacy characters L-R: [[Shado Vao]], [[Konrad Rus]], [[Wolf Sazen]], [[Antares Draco]], [[Sigel Dare]], [[Ganner Krieg]], [[Marasiah Fel]], [[Roan Fel]], [[Astraal Vao]], [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker's]] [[Force ghost/Legends|Force ghost]], [[Deliah Blue]], [[Cade Skywalker]], [[Jariah Syn]], [[Darth Krayt]], [[Darth Talon]] & [[Darth Nihl]].]]The series was set to debut around the same time as the first installment in the ''Legacy of the Force'' novel series, which led to some speculation that the two series would be connected. Though the first outline of a then-nameless ''Legacy of the Force'' was delivered five months prior to the first outline of ''Legacy'',<ref>{{JCFCite|url=posts/29031346|board=Literature|thread=WWDRD? Del Ray and the Legacy Line|user=[[Randy Stradley|Randy_Stradley]]|date=2008-08-23|time=6:51 pm|archivedate=20200802095521}}</ref> Ostrander and Duursema actually came up with the idea of using the word "Legacy" first. When ''Legacy of the Force'' came up as a title during brainstorming sessions, the idea of creating the [[Legacy era]] was born.<ref>{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=222689&start=2175#14968484|thread=Books, Comics, & Television VIPs|user=[[Leland Chee|Tasty Taste]]|date=2006-06-06|time=12:53 PM}}</ref>

====Crafting the galaxy====
{{Quote|SW is epic and epic things must occur.|John Ostrander|<ref name="JCF Epic">{{JCFCite|url=posts/23169823|board=Literature|thread=LEGACY #0|user=jfostrander|date=2006-02-15|time=7:09 am|archivedate=20230724185945}}</ref>}}
*According to John Ostrander, launching the series required a [[Darth Krayt|major villain]] and a [[Sith–Imperial War|major occurrence]], as he believes that that is what drives ''Star Wars'' overall.<ref name="JCF Epic" /> He, and all of the parties responsible for planning ''Legacy'', agreed early on that a Skywalker was needed in order to appeal to as many fans as possible.<ref>{{JCFCite|url=posts/24338956|board=Literature|thread=Legacy #1: Broken, part 1 (of 6) (release discussion)|user=[[John Ostrander|jfostrander]]|date=2006-07-10|time=7:28 am|archivedate=20200801163259}}</ref>

*Ostrander has also stated that the concept of the Empire that he and Jan Duursema used grew from their concept of reading the Expanded Universe through to ''[[Star Wars: The Dark Nest Trilogy]]'' --- an Empire that has changed and evolved from [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] era.<ref>{{JCFCite|url=posts/24365597|board=Literature|thread=Legacy: That's it, I'm out|user=[[John Ostrander|jfostrander]]|date=2006-07-13|time=10:42 am|archivedate=20200801190135}}</ref>

*Jan Duursema has stated, perhaps jokingly, that while naming Cade, she was looking for a name that she could hear [[Beru Whitesun Lars/Legends|Aunt Beru]] calling.<ref>{{JCFCite|url=posts/23142025|board=Literature|thread=The Official Legacy Series Discussion Thread|user=[[Jan Duursema|Jan_Duursema]]|date=2006-02-12|time=2:06 pm|archivedate=20200801160544}}</ref><ref name="Legacy 2006 39">Ironically, in [[Legacy (2006) 39|''Legacy'' (2006) 39]], Cade undergoes a sequence in which he is reliving Luke's past at the Homestead, and Aunt Beru calls both Luke and Cade.</ref>

==Release and reception==
[[File:Talonfan.jpg|thumb|right|200px|One of many Darth Talon fan costumes at [[Celebration IV]].]]
Issues of Legacy routinely sell over 30,000 copies.<ref>{{WebCite|url=http://www.cbgxtra.com/Default.aspx?tabid=2203|text=May 2007 Estimates of Diamond Comics Sales|work=Comics Buyers Guide|archivedate=20071028063926}}</ref><ref>{{WebCite|url=http://www.cbgxtra.com/Default.aspx?tabid=2454|text=November 2007 Estimates of Diamond Comics Sales|work=Comics Buyers Guide|archivedate=20071228225654}}</ref>

Some fans were disappointed in the first issues, and were still largely hesitant about the series. Ostrander and Duursema continued to address fans' concerns; Ostrander stated: "I certainly don't expect everyone to love Legacy."<ref>{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=245230&start=1020#15199061|thread=Star Wars: Legacy comic series SPOILERS! (JFA)|user=[[John Ostrander|JohnOstrander]]|date=2007-07-06|time=3:59 PM}}</ref> He also said that while it was fair to criticize Duursema and him on the value of their work, it was not to do so if only because they went a different path that what some fans speculated.<ref>{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=245230&start=1020#15199150|thread=Star Wars: Legacy comic series SPOILERS! (JFA)|user=[[John Ostrander|JohnOstrander]]|date=2007-07-06|time=4:12 PM|quote=Criticizing what Jan and I DO is valid. Speculating on what we're GOING to do and then criticizing us on that basis, in my opinion, is not. Legacy may not be how you see the SW Universe or what you want to see from it and that's perfectly fair. But Jan and I love SW, too. And we have a lot of experience at what we do. We've put a HUGE amount of time and labor into developing Legacy. To suggest otherwise, IMO, is NOT fair.}}</ref>

==Continuity==
Set in 137 ABY, ''Legacy'' takes place later than any other events in the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] timeline. It features numerous references to earlier Expanded Universe works.

===References to Dark Horse's ''Republic'' comics===
Both Ostrander and Duursema were heavily involved with ''[[Star Wars: Republic]]'', and several characters and other elements from that series reappeared in ''Legacy''.
[[File:KKruhkCade-Legacy2006-11.jpg|thumb|right|250px|K'Kruhk appears to Cade Skywalker on Ossus.]]

*'''[[Darth Krayt|A'Sharad Hett]]:''' Hett was a Jedi who was active in the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], and was featured in many issues of ''Republic''. He was a confirmed survivor of [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]],<ref name="The New Essential Chronology">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> and in ''Legacy'', was revealed to be the man behind the mask of Darth Krayt.<ref name="Legacy 15: Claws of the Dragon part 2">[[Legacy (2006) 15|''Legacy'' (2006) 15]]</ref> The story of how Hett fell to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] and assumed the mantle of the Sith Lord was told in the ''Legacy'' arc ''Claws of the Dragon''.

*'''[[K'Kruhk]]:''' An [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Old Republic]] era Jedi who, like Hett, was featured in many issues of ''Republic'', K'Kruhk was also a confirmed survivor of Order 66.<ref name="The New Essential Chronology" /> He met Cade Skywalker on [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]], revealing to the reader that he had reemerged from hiding some time prior to the Sith-Imperial War to join the [[New Jedi Order]].<ref name="Legacy 11: Ghosts part 1">[[Legacy (2006) 11|''Legacy'' (2006) 11]]</ref>

*'''[[Chak (Wookiee)|Chak]]:''' A member of the long-lived [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] species, Chak made a brief appearance in the final story arc of ''Republic'', [[Star Wars: Republic: Hidden Enemy|''Hidden Enemy'']].<ref name="Republic: The Hidden Enemy">''[[Star Wars: Republic: Hidden Enemy]]''</ref> He was revealed to be a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] and the partner of [[Kee]] in [[Star Wars: Legacy (2006) 0|Issue 0]].<ref name="Star Wars: Legacy 0">[[Star Wars: Legacy (2006) 0|''Star Wars: Legacy'' (2006) 0]]''</ref>

*'''[[Darth Andeddu]]:''' A [[Holocron/Legends|holocron]] of the ancient [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] first appeared in [[Republic 63|''Republic'' 63]],<ref name="Republic 63: Striking From the Shadows">[[Republic 63|''Republic'' 63]]</ref> and later appeared to Darth Krayt in [[Legacy (2006) 5|Issue #4]] of [[Star Wars: Legacy: Broken|Broken]].<ref name="Legacy 5: Broken, Part 4">[[Legacy (2006) 5|''Legacy'' (2006) 5]]</ref> He was vanquished by Darth Wyyrlok in Legacy 27: Into the Core.

*'''[[Quinlan Vos/Legends|Quinlan Vos]]:''' He was mentioned by Darth Krayt.<ref name="Legacy 17: Claws of the Dragon, Part 4">[[Legacy (2006) 17|''Legacy'' (2006) 17]]</ref>

*'''[[The Wheel/Legends|The Wheel]]:''' Originally a creation of [[Archie Goodwin]] in the pages of [[Star Wars (1977)|Marvel Star Wars]],<ref name="Star Wars 18: The Empire Strikes">[[Star Wars (1977) 18|''Star Wars'' (1977) 18]]</ref> ''The Wheel'' was reused by Ostrander and Duursema in [[Republic 49|Issue #49]] of ''Republic''.<ref name="''Republic'' 49: ''Sacrifice''">[[Republic 49|''Republic'' 49]]</ref> It was most recently seen in the [[Star Wars: Legacy: Trust Issues|Trust Issues]] arc of ''Legacy''.<ref name="Legacy: Trust Issues">''[[Star Wars: Legacy: Trust Issues]]''</ref>

*'''[[T'ra Saa/Legends|T'ra Saa]]:''' The [[Neti/Legends|Neti]] Jedi Master who routinely appeared in the pages of ''Republic'', like K'Kruhk, survived well into the [[Legacy era]], and sat on the [[Master's Council|Jedi High Council]] in the [[Hidden Temple]].<ref name="Legacy 25: The Hidden Temple, Part 1" />

*'''[[Tholme/Legends|Tholme]]:''' Another Jedi Master who also regularly appeared in ''Republic'', also survived Order 66. He was mentioned having passed away at some point after that.<ref name="Legacy 25: The Hidden Temple, Part 1" />

===References to Bantam and Del Rey===
Although ''Legacy'' was designed to be enjoyed independently of any ''Star Wars'' novels,<ref name="SWBoards 2190" /> the state of the galaxy in 137 ABY still draws on many elements from the post-[[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Return of the Jedi'']] novels published by [[Bantam Spectra]] and [[Del Rey]], which shaped the state of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] following the death of [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]].
[[File:LegacyVong.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Yuuzhan Vong attack Cade Skywalker.]]

*'''The [[Yuuzhan Vong War]]:''' Chronicled in the pages of [[Star Wars: The New Jedi Order|''The New Jedi Order'']]<ref name="The New Jedi Order">''[[Star Wars: The New Jedi Order]]''</ref> ([[1999]] - [[2003]]), the Yuuzhan Vong War wrought devastation on the galaxy from [[25 ABY/Legends|25]] – [[29 ABY/Legends|29 ABY]]. In ''Legacy'', the defeated [[Yuuzhan Vong|invaders]] have been used as pawns by Darth Krayt's [[One Sith|Sith Order]] in order to start the Sith–Imperial War, a war that was in part fueled by wide-scale galactic mistrust of the Vong.<ref name="Legacy 1: Broken Part 1" />

*'''The [[Fel dynasty]]:''' [[Roan Fel]], Emperor of the [[Fel Empire|New Galactic Empire]], is a confirmed descendant of [[Soontir Fel/Legends|Baron Soontir Fel]].<ref name="Star Wars: The Ultimate Visual Guide#Special Edition">''[[Star Wars: The Ultimate Visual Guide]]''</ref> He and his daughter [[Marasiah Fel|Marasiah]] both have white forelocks, suggesting that they are possible descendants of [[Jagged Fel]]. This was confirmed in [[Legacy (2006) 33|Issue 33]], which confirmed that Jagged Fel was Roan Fel's grandfather.

*'''The [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]]:''' Prior to their displacement by [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire]], the reigning galactic power was the Galactic Federation of Free Alliances, which was established in [[2002]]'s ''[[The New Jedi Order: Destiny's Way]]''.<ref name="Destiny's Way">''[[The New Jedi Order: Destiny's Way]]''</ref>

*'''[[Bastion/Legends|Bastion]]:''' The seat of power of Roan Fel's Empire, Bastion was established as the capital of the [[Imperial Remnant]] in [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]], in [[1997]]'s ''[[Specter of the Past]]''.<ref name="Specter of the Past">''[[Specter of the Past]]''</ref>

*'''[[Pellaeon-class Star Destroyer]]:''' The massive Imperial ship is named in honor of the former leader of the Imperial Remnant, [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]].<ref name="Star Wars: Legacy 0" />

*'''The [[Jedi Academy (Ossus)|Jedi Temple]]:''' The [[New Jedi Order]] is headquartered on [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]]; their temple first appeared in [[35 ABY/Legends|35 ABY]], in [[2005]]'s [[Dark Nest I: The Joiner King|''The Joiner King'']].<ref name="The Joiner King">''[[Dark Nest I: The Joiner King]]''</ref>

*'''[[Mara Jade Skywalker]]:''' The wife of [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], Mara appeared as a [[Death stick/Legends|death stick]]-induced hallucination to Cade Skywalker on Ossus.<ref name="Legacy 11: Ghosts part 1" />

*'''[[Darth Caedus]]:''' Darth Krayt referenced Darth Caedus's [[Second Galactic Civil War|conquest of the galaxy]] while revealing his origins to Cade Skywalker.<ref name="Star Wars: Legacy 18: Claws of the Dragon, Part 5">[[Legacy (2006) 18|''Legacy'' (2006) 18]]</ref> Writer John Ostrander was referencing ''[[Star Wars: Legacy of the Force]]'', which was being published simultaneously with ''[[Star Wars: Legacy: Claws of the Dragon]]'' in [[2007]].

*'''[[Vergere]]:''' A major character of ''The New Jedi Order'', Vergere appeared in a flashback while Darth Krayt revealed his origins to Cade Skywalker.<ref name="Star Wars: Legacy 18: Claws of the Dragon, Part 5" /> It was revealed that she tutored Krayt while keeping him in the [[Embrace of Pain]], just as she did to [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]] in [[2002]]'s ''[[The New Jedi Order: Traitor]]''.<ref name="Traitor">''[[The New Jedi Order: Traitor]]''</ref>

*'''The [[Codru-Ji]]:''' First introduced in [[1994]]'s ''[[The Crystal Star]]'',<ref name="The Crystal Star">''[[The Crystal Star]]''</ref> the Codru-Ji, and their home planet of [[Munto Codru/Legends|Munto Codru]], play a large role in ''Legacy''{{'s}} [[Legacy (2006) 13|''Ready to Die'']].<ref name="Star Wars: Legacy 13: Ready to Die">[[Legacy (2006) 13|''Legacy'' (2006) 13]]</ref>

Many fans expressed their desire to see Jedi from Luke Skywalker's order appear in ''Legacy'', such as [[Lowbacca]], a member of the long-lived [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] species. John Ostrander replied to these claims, stating that using a character from that era would compromise what the authors of Del Rey's novels could do with them.<ref>{{JCFCite|url=posts/29499575|board=Literature|thread=where were K'Kruhk and T'ra Saa all this time?|user=[[John Ostrander|jfostrander01]]|date=2008-11-24|time=7:05 am|archivedate=20200802100713}}</ref> However, he also stated that if the creative teams doing the novels had no more interest in a particular character, he would be up for using them.<ref>{{JCFCite|url=posts/29503732|board=Literature|thread=where were K'Kruhk and T'ra Saa all this time?|user=[[John Ostrander|jfostrander01]]|date=2008-11-25|time=7:05 am|archivedate=20200802100713}}</ref>

===References to other media===
*'''[[Darth Bane/Legends|Darth Bane]] and [[Darth Nihilus]]:''' [[File:BattleOfDevilsCrevasse-Legacy33.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The [[Sea Leviathan]], attacking Mon [[Dac/Legends|Calamari]].]]The two ancient Sith Lords, both well-established in the [[Star Wars Legends|Expanded Universe]], saw their holocrons accessed by Darth Krayt.<ref name="Legacy 5: Broken, Part 4" />

*'''[[Nagai/Legends|Nagai]] and [[Zeltron/Legends|Zeltrons]]:''' Two prominent species from [[Star Wars (1977)|Marvel Star Wars]], which were largely unseen in the Expanded Universe for over two decades, are represented by Darth Nihl and Deliah Blue, respectively.

*'''[[Aquatic assault stormtrooper]]s and [[Aquatic Terrain Armored Transport|AT-AT swimmers]]:''' These types of soldiers and vehicles from [[Star Wars (1977)|Marvel Star Wars]] and [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game|RPG materials]] respectively, which were a rare appearance in the Expanded Universe for over two decades, see action under the service of [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire]]. Seatroopers from [[Alpha Squadron (seatrooper)|Alpha Squadron]] were encountered by [[Imperial Knight]] [[Treis Sinde]] during the [[Genocide on Dac]]. Sith [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] [[Vul Isen]] and [[Darth Azard]] used an AT-AT Swimmer in coordination with the [[Sithspawn/Legends|sithspawn]] [[Sea Leviathan]] during the [[Battle of Devil's Crevasse]].<ref name="Legacy 32: Fight Another Day, Part 1">[[Legacy (2006) 32|''Legacy'' (2006) 32]]</ref>

*'''[[Leviathan (creature)|Leviathans]]:''' These serpentine [[Sithspawn/Legends|Sithspawn]] made their first debut in [[Star Wars: Jedi Academy: Leviathan|''Jedi Academy: Leviathan'']] which is set during the [[New Republic era]]. Later Expanded Universe sources including ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' placed the roots of their origins as the creations of [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] dissidents during the [[Hundred-Year Darkness/Legends|Hundred-Year Darkness]].<ref name="Legacy 30: Vector, Part 11">[[Legacy (2006) 30|''Legacy'' (2006) 30]]</ref> Sith scientist [[Vul Isen]] created a [[Sea Leviathan|specially-adapted marine version]] of the Leviathan which preyed specifically on [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] and also absorbed their sentience.<ref name="Legacy 33: Fight Another Day, Part 2">[[Legacy (2006) 33|''Legacy'' (2006) 33]]</ref>

===Continuity errors===
Issue 0 of ''Legacy'' made reference to the [[Rings of Coruscant]], which was possibly intended as a reference to the rings Coruscant received in ''[[The New Jedi Order: Traitor]]'', but which disappeared after ''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]''.

[[Darth Bane's holocron]] was shown to have an [[Orbalisk armor|Orbalisk Armored]] Gatekeeper. This, however, contradicts with ''[[Darth Bane: Rule of Two]]''{{'s}} account of Bane having his armor removed prior to succeeding in crafting a holocron. It is later explained in ''[[Darth Bane: Dynasty of Evil]]'' that Bane chose to represent himself in his Orbalisk armor, in an effort to inspire fear and to conceal a majority of his true face.

===Crossovers===
====Legacy of the Force====
Darth Krayt's Sith Empire appears in the nine-book series ''Legacy of the Force'', which began its publishing run just one month before ''Legacy''.<ref name="Betrayal">''[[Legacy of the Force: Betrayal]]''</ref> In ''[[Legacy of the Force: Tempest]]'', released in November 2006, [[Lumiya]] hints to [[Alema Rar]] that there may be other Sith in the galaxy.<ref name="Tempest">''[[Legacy of the Force: Tempest]]''</ref> Later, in ''[[Legacy of the Force: Inferno]]'', released in August 2007, Alema discovers these Sith on Korriban, who identify themselves to her as the One Sith, a name that would not be used in ''Legacy'' until [[Legacy (2006) 27|Issue 27]],<ref name="Legacy 27: Into the Core" /> released one year later. The Sith tell Alema that they have had contact with both Lumiya and Vergere, and give her a [[Holocron/Legends|holocron]] made by [[Darth Vectivus]] to give to Jacen Solo.<ref name="Inferno">''[[Legacy of the Force: Inferno]]''</ref>

In ''[[Legacy of the Force: Fury]]'', released in November 2007, the One Sith make their final appearance within ''Legacy of the Force'', sending the Sith [[Dician]] to destroy Lumiya's [[The Home|Asteroid Base]]. Though Dician's ship, the [[Poison Moon (starship)|''Poison Moon'']], has contact with the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], Dician's identity is not discovered, and the One Sith remain a background threat. Alema Rar meets her fate in ''Fury'', preventing the possibility of knowledge of the One Sith's existence from spreading.<ref name="Fury">''[[Legacy of the Force: Fury]]''</ref> [[File:Celeste_profile.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Celeste Morne in ''Legacy''.]]

John Ostrander stated that it was the choice of the ''Legacy of the Force'' planning team to include the One Sith.<ref>{{JCFCite|url=posts/29026457|board=Literature|thread=WWDRD? Del Ray and the Legacy Line|user=[[John Ostrander|jfostrander01]]|date=2008-08-22|time=10:24 pm|archivedate=20200802095308}}</ref>

====Crosscurrent====
The One Sith makes a brief appearance in ''[[Crosscurrent]]'', released in January 2010, when the [[Anzat (species)|Anzat]] [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]] [[Kell Douro]] travels to [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]] and is instructed by [[Darth Wyyrlok (I)|Darth Wyyrlok I]] to go and keep tabs on [[Phaegon III's largest moon]]. When Douro requests to speak with Darth Krayt directly, Wyyrlok informs him that Krayt is in stasis.<ref name="Crosscurrent">''[[Crosscurrent]]''</ref>

====Vector====
''Legacy'' is a part of ''[[Star Wars: Vector]]'', the twelve-part crossover story arc which involves all four of Dark Horse's then-current ''Star Wars'' comics. As the latest comic series in the chronology, ''Legacy'' was allotted the final four of the twelve issues. Within these issues, [[Celeste Morne]], the protagonist of ''Vector'', is discovered by Cade Skywalker, and joins him on his quest to assassinate Darth Krayt.<ref name="Star Wars: Legacy 28: Vector, Part 9">[[Legacy (2006) 28|''Legacy'' (2006) 28]]</ref>

====Fate of the Jedi====
Darth Krayt was featured in [[Fate of the Jedi: Apocalypse]], the last novel in the [[Star Wars: Fate of the Jedi|Fate of the Jedi]] series. Darth Krayt is aware of [[Abeloth]] and fears she can pose a threat to his plans. Krayt traveled [[Beyond shadows|Beyond Shadows]] and fought together with [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] against Abeloth.<ref name="Apocalypse">''[[Fate of the Jedi: Apocalypse]]''</ref>

==Legacy==
===Miniatures===
[[File:Roanminiature.jpg|thumb|left|150px|The Roan Fel miniature.]]
The [[Legacy of the Force (Star Wars Miniatures)|Legacy of the Force]] set of [[Star Wars Miniatures]], released in early [[2008]], features miniatures of Darth Krayt, Darth Nihl, Darth Talon, [[Antares Draco]], Roan Fel, [[Ganner Krieg]], [[Sigel Dare]], Marasiah Fel, Morlish Veed, Nyna Calixte, Shado Vao, Wolf Sazen, Cade Skywalker, Deliah Blue, and Jariah Syn.<ref>{{WebCite|url=http://www.miniaturetrading.com/im/selectCard/series10254|text=Legacy of the Force at Miniature Trading|work=Miniature Trading|archivedate=20080517034311}}</ref>

The miniatures set was advertised through a one-page comic ad produced by [[Wizards of the Coast]] and drawn by Jan Duursema, which appeared on the back covers of ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' issues [[Star Wars Insider 100|100]],<ref name="starwarscom">{{SWArchive|url=gaming/other/rpg/news20080522.html|text=Big Nostalgia in Miniatures Ad}}</ref> [[Star Wars Insider 102|102]]<ref name="TheForce.net">{{WebCite|url=http://www.theforce.net/comics/story/New_Wizards_Of_The_Coast_Ad_112650.asp|text=New Wizards Of The Coast Ad|work=[[TheForce.net]]|archivedate=20200618194820}}</ref> and [[Star Wars Insider 103|103]],<ref name="Star Wars Insider 103">[[Star Wars Insider 103|''Star Wars Insider'' 103]]</ref> and also in a number of Dark Horse comics.<ref name="TheForce.net" />

===Action figures===
[[File:Cadetalonfigures.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Darth Talon and Cade Skywalker Hasbro figures.]]
Also in 2008, [[Hasbro]], as part of its Legacy Collection comic packs, released three comic packs featuring characters from ''Legacy'' in 3 3/4 inch action figures.<ref>{{Rebelscum|url=TLCcomicpacks.asp|text=Star Wars The Legacy Collection Photo Archives}}</ref> Cade Skywalker and Darth Talon appeared in a two-pack along with [[Legacy (2006) 2|Issue 2]] of ''Broken'', while Imperial Knights Ganner Krieg and Antares Draco appeared in a two-pack along with [[Legacy (2006) 6|Issue 5]] of ''Broken'', and Darth Krayt and Imperial Knight Sigel Dare appeared in a two-pack along with [[Legacy (2006) 22|''The Wrath of the Dragon'']].

An Entertainment Earth exclusive [[Joker Squad]] set was made available in April [[2009]], including the Sith [[Darth Maleval]].<ref>{{WebCite|url=https://www.entertainmentearth.com/product/star-wars-joker-squad-stormtrooper-figures-an-ee-exclusive/hs87923|text=EE Exclusive Star Wars Joker Squad Action Figures|archivedate=20200920121106}}</ref>
